.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8004:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s browse surfaces are real and useful, but its own docs now admit the “+34% palace boost” framing was misleading, and its benchmark history shows taxonomy-first routing can crater recall: global LLM routing hit 34.2% R@5, while even the better summary-based room routing still trails the hybrid no-rooms baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8005:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not refactor `memory_search` or `memory_context` into room-first retrieval. If we add taxonomy, it should help an agent orient or narrow after the fact, while core ranking stays query-led and hybrid.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8006:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8007:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8009:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8011:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s layer stack is conceptually neat, but it duplicates search/filter logic already present in `searcher.py`, emits formatted text rather than one authoritative structured result shape, and still uses rough token heuristics (`len(...) // 4`). Public already has profile-aware orchestration and budget enforcement in `memory_context`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8012:- **Why it matters for us**: For Public, a separate wake-up engine would duplicate ranking, result formatting, and token-budget logic we already solved elsewhere. If we want L0/L1-like behavior, it should be expressed as an existing `memory_context`/`session_bootstrap` profile, not as a parallel retrieval subsystem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8013:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8014:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8016:### Finding 5: Public should preserve its modular handler architecture and not absorb new memory features into a single giant server file
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8018:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s `mcp_server.py` owns tool registration, bootstrap text, drawer writes, KG writes, diary writes, and JSON-RPC dispatch in one file. Public splits orchestration, retrieval, save validation, and server lifecycle into dedicated modules.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8019:- **Why it matters for us**: The next wave of ideas here is taxonomy, hooks, and maybe identity UX. A monolithic merge would make write authority, governance, and review harder. The safer direction is to add any new capabilities as bounded handlers layered on top of the existing validated save/search contracts.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8020:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8021:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:1:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:3:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: On agent integration and project safety, Public is ahead. MemPalace contributes a cleaner, more memorable behavioral surface, but not a safer one. The transferable parts are the small ones: one explicit memory protocol surface, and harness-level passive save timing. The scoping and multi-agent model should not be imported wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:6:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:80:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:151:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:152:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:153:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:164:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:165:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:166:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:263:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:265:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:267:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:268:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:269:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:270:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:651:     3	- **What it does**: Source-confirmed. Writes are logged to a JSONL WAL before execution, but drawer removal is still a real `col.delete()`, dedup physically deletes near-duplicates, and `repair.py` scans, prunes corrupt IDs, or fully rebuilds the collection after extracting all drawers.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:652:     4	- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace has useful operational repair instincts, especially around vector-store corruption, but not a soft-delete or retention model. For us, the reusable part is repair tooling and pre-rebuild backup discipline, not the delete semantics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:653:     5	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:654:     6	- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:658:    10	- **What it does**: Source-confirmed. Public reconsolidates on save, archives superseded rows instead of overwriting them, records `logical_key`, `version_number`, `valid_to`, and `superseded_by_memory_id`, repairs active projections when logical identity drifts, and keeps an append-only `mutation_ledger` guarded by triggers against update/delete.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:659:    11	- **Why it matters for us**: On memory hygiene specifically, Public is already beyond MemPalace. We should not import MemPalace’s drawer lifecycle wholesale; we should only cherry-pick targeted ideas like offline corruption repair or a canonical topic-key layer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:660:    12	- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:661:    13	- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:738:    90	What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:809:   161	- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:810:   162	- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:811:   163	- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:822:   174	- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:823:   175	- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:824:   176	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4221:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/benchmarks/HYBRID_MODE.md:536:Larger candidate pool gives keyword re-ranking more to work with. If the answer is at position 45 semantically but has perfect keyword overlap, we need it in the pool to promote it. Cost: ChromaDB returns slightly more data per query. Impact on speed: negligible.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4558:   176	**What it does:** Stores verbatim session text. Embeds with sentence transformers. Retrieves by cosine similarity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5278:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5279:### Finding 1: Shipped MemPalace retrieval is still a flat single-collection Chroma query; the stronger hybrid/rerank story mostly lives in benchmark paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5281:- **What it does**: The MCP-facing `mempalace_search` tool delegates straight to `search_memories()`, which only issues `col.query(query_texts, n_results, include, optional where)` against `mempalace_drawers` and returns text, wing, room, source file, and `1 - distance`. The README’s April 7 note explicitly says the 100% rerank story is real but “not in the public benchmark scripts,” and the shipped search benchmarks still exercise `search_memories()` directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5282:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s benchmark ceiling should not be mistaken for its agent-runtime retrieval behavior. Public already ships the stronger live retrieval stack in production: hybrid search, rerank, trace, scope normalization, and specialized routing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5283:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5284:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5286:### Finding 2: Wing and room retrieval are useful browse handles, but L2 is metadata filtering, not a competing retrieval engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5288:- **What it does**: `Layer2.retrieve()` uses `col.get()` with optional `where` filters on `wing` and `room`; if no filter is passed, it just fetches drawers without a query. `tool_list_rooms()` and taxonomy tools likewise count metadata rather than ranking relevance.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5289:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest retrieval idea MemPalace actually ships for agent ergonomics: memorable human-facing narrowing. It is good browse UX, but it does not replace semantic memory search, semantic code search, or structural graph lookup.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5290:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5291:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5293:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 “wake-up stack” is mostly presentation policy over one store, not four distinct retrieval substrates
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5295:- **What it does**: L1 scans the whole drawer collection in batches, sorts by `importance`/`emotional_weight`/`weight`, groups by room, truncates to 15 snippets and 3200 chars, and emits compact text. L3 then reuses the same Chroma query path as `searcher.py`. The “layers” mostly change formatting, caps, and filters.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5296:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable part is not a new retrieval algorithm. It is a good prompt-packaging pattern for startup and compaction survival: cheap identity, compact essential story, topic browse, deep search. Public could borrow the packaging shape without downgrading the actual retrieval backend.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5297:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5298:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5300:### Finding 4: Palace graph traversal is topic-neighborhood navigation from metadata, not a substitute for code-graph or causal retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5302:- **What it does**: MemPalace builds a graph by scanning drawer metadata, treating rooms as nodes and shared wings/halls as edges, then traverses by shared wing overlap with a simple BFS and fuzzy room-name matching. Public’s structural routing, by contrast, explicitly sends semantic code queries to CocoIndex and structural dependency questions to `code_graph_query`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5303:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s graph is useful for “nearby topics” in memory. It cannot answer callers, imports, dependency blast radius, or code ownership questions the way Public’s code-graph can, and it is also different from Public’s causal memory lineage.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5304:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5305:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5307:### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval stack is already better factored and more inspectable than MemPalace’s single-surface search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5309:- **What it does**: Public’s `memory_search` is hybrid by default, normalizes scope, traces retrieval, exposes rerank metadata, and binds progressive disclosure to scope-aware cursors. `context-server.ts` advertises channel availability and routes semantic code search to CocoIndex and structural queries to `code_graph_query`. The compact-code-graph plugin injects deduped transport blocks during message and compaction phases.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5310:- **Why it matters for us**: On retrieval architecture, Public should not collapse back to a single “drawer search” surface. MemPalace contributes a memorable vocabulary and a good browse mental model, but Public already has the better substrate split: memory retrieval, semantic code retrieval, structural code retrieval, and transport-safe continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5311:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5312:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5354:## Findings

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:224:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:225:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7210:**What it does:** Stores verbatim session text. Embeds with sentence transformers. Retrieves by cosine similarity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10441:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10442:### Finding 1: MemPalace is really four subsystems around one shared local store boundary
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10444:- **What it does**: The package exposes one `mempalace` CLI entrypoint, then fans into project/conversation ingest, ChromaDB drawer retrieval, a separate SQLite temporal KG, and hook-triggered save timing. The real runtime split is: `cli.py` dispatches commands, `palace.py` owns Chroma collection access, `knowledge_graph.py` owns fact storage, and `hooks_cli.py` controls preservation timing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10445:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a clean architectural separation: verbatim corpus, fact graph, and save-timing automation are not collapsed into one retrieval layer. Public already separates semantic memory, code search, and code graph; MemPalace reinforces that this split is a strength, not accidental complexity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10446:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10447:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10449:### Finding 2: The raw-verbatim posture is source-confirmed, but “closets” are mostly product language in this version
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10451:- **What it does**: The code really does store drawer chunks as verbatim text in `mempalace_drawers` and search those drawers directly. But there is no first-class closet store in the implementation path I traced; L1 is generated on the fly from top drawers, and deep search goes straight against Chroma. The README’s closet language is more conceptual than storage-structural right now.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10452:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful part is not “closets” as branding; it is raw retention plus lightweight derived views. Public can borrow the posture without copying the metaphor.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10453:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10454:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10456:### Finding 3: Agent discipline is embedded into the MCP surface, not left to prompts alone
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10458:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, so the first read call doubles as behavioral bootstrapping. The server also cleanly splits read tools, write tools, navigation tools, KG tools, and diary tools, with stderr logging and a write-ahead log for writes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10459:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has stronger recovery surfaces (`session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context`), but MemPalace is better at making the memory protocol agent-visible right where the agent starts. That could improve recall ergonomics without inventing a new retrieval backend.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10460:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10461:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10463:### Finding 4: The wake-up stack is real, but the implementation is simpler than the README framing
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10465:- **What it does**: L0 is just `~/.mempalace/identity.txt`. L1 is not a maintained summary layer; it is regenerated from the highest-weight drawers, grouped by room. L2 is metadata-filtered `get()`, and L3 is semantic `query()`. So the stack exists, but only L0/L1 are “wake-up” in practice; the rest are normal retrieval modes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10466:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s session recovery is already more explicit and richer than this, but MemPalace contributes a good mental model: cheap always-on identity plus a bounded always-on story layer before full retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10467:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10468:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10470:### Finding 5: Palace graph traversal and temporal KG are separate systems with different truth models
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10472:- **What it does**: `palace_graph.py` derives room tunnels from Chroma metadata only; it is a navigational overlay, not a fact database. `knowledge_graph.py` is a separate SQLite triple store with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, explicit invalidation, and timelines. They solve different problems and are intentionally not fused.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10473:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a strong pattern for Public: keep verbatim memory provenance and fact-like temporal assertions separate, so invalidation and fact drift do not contaminate raw session evidence.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10474:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10475:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10477:### Finding 6: Ingestion is not “store everything untouched”; it is chunk-and-route with heuristic restructuring on some paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10479:- **What it does**: Project ingest chunks files into overlapping ~800-char drawers and routes them by folder/name/keyword heuristics. Conversation ingest chunks by exchange pair or paragraph fallback. `general_extractor.py` goes further and classifies text into decisions, preferences, milestones, problems, and emotions. So raw retention is true at the drawer level, but not at the “unaltered original session object” level.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10480:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful lesson is selective restructuring by ingest mode, not a literal “never transform anything” dogma. Public should compare this carefully against `generate-context.js`, which preserves curated session semantics instead of blindly chunking transcripts.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10481:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10482:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10484:### Finding 7: Hook-driven preservation is a timing architecture, not a storage architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10486:- **What it does**: The stop hook blocks every 15 human messages; the precompact hook always blocks. The shell and Python paths both use “block with a reason” so the agent performs the save, optionally followed by auto-ingest. This architecture protects context before loss without needing a richer storage model.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10487:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has compaction transport via the compact-code-graph plugin, but MemPalace has a stronger explicit checkpointing posture. The idea worth stealing is the enforced preservation checkpoint, not the exact bash implementation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10488:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10489:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10491:### Finding 8: Public already beats MemPalace on session bootstrap sophistication, but MemPalace beats Public on memory protocol ergonomics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10493:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. MemPalace is simpler, but its status/bootstrap surface is better at teaching the agent when to query, verify, write diaries, and invalidate facts.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10494:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture takeaway is asymmetric. Public should not copy MemPalace’s backend wholesale. It should selectively import raw-verbatim retention ideas, checkpoint hooks, and protocol-forward memory UX.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10495:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10496:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10543:## Findings

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10595:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. MemPalace is simpler, but its status/bootstrap surface is better at teaching the agent when to query, verify, write diaries, and invalidate facts.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10596:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture takeaway is asymmetric. Public should not copy MemPalace’s backend wholesale. It should selectively import raw-verbatim retention ideas, checkpoint hooks, and protocol-forward memory UX.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10597:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10598:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:1:### Finding 5: Identity should be governed internally and human-friendly externally
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:3:- **What it does**: MemPalace makes people/projects/wings a first-class user-facing taxonomy from the start. Public instead grounds retrieval in governed scope fields like `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: The shift is not “copy wings.” It is to split identity into two planes: machine-trustworthy internal scope and optional human-friendly aliases/navigation. That would give us MemPalace’s ergonomic browsing without weakening governed retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:5:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:8:### Finding 6: Truthfulness boundaries are part of the architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s own correction note shows how easily mode boundaries blur: raw mode, rooms mode, AAAK, and unreleased rerank paths were marketed too closely together. Public already encodes stricter guardrails in structured save contracts and confidence floors.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: A memory system is not trustworthy if its interfaces blur what is stored, what is derived, what is experimental, and what is benchmark-only. Evidence tiering needs to be part of the product contract, not just a doc habit.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:12:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:74:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:145:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:146:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:147:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:158:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:159:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:160:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:257:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:259:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:261:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:262:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:263:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:264:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6915:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6916:### Finding 1: MemPalace’s strongest pattern is lifecycle-enforced memory discipline, not just retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6919:- **What it does**: The system teaches and enforces a full memory loop: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`; that protocol tells the agent to check memory before answering, write a diary after each session, and invalidate stale facts; the wake-up stack injects L0+L1 startup context; the Stop hook blocks every 15 user turns; the PreCompact hook always blocks before shrinkage. End-to-end, the agent is told what memory is for, when to read it, and when it must preserve state.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6920:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the real differentiator. Public already has stronger save authority and recovery authority than MemPalace, via explicit-target JSON-primary saves, `session_bootstrap`, and compaction transport. What Public still lacks is the same enforced timing: a bounded preservation checkpoint before stop/compaction, plus one memory-facing bootstrap surface that teaches query/save discipline as directly as MemPalace does.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6921:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6922:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6924:### Finding 2: The “4-layer stack” is mostly an operating scaffold, not a novel retrieval engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6927:- **What it does**: L1 is a top-15, metadata-weighted snippet pack from the same Chroma collection; L2 is not semantic search at all, it is a filtered `get` by wing/room; only L3 actually performs semantic query. So the stack is valuable, but mostly as an escalation ladder for context cost, not as a fundamentally different retrieval substrate.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6928:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should borrow the cheap-startup-plus-escalation idea, not the marketing frame that this is a deep new retrieval method. A thin startup digest and explicit “when to escalate” policy would fit Public well; copying the full L0/L1/L2/L3 language as if it were a distinct retrieval breakthrough would be hype-prone.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6929:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6930:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6932:### Finding 3: “Store everything” is real, but ingest is still routed, chunked, and sometimes extracted
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6935:- **What it does**: Project files are chunked into 800-character drawers with overlap and room routing; convo mode chunks by exchange pairs; “general” mode stops being raw filing and instead heuristically extracts five memory classes. So the system keeps verbatim text, but it still normalizes, routes, segments, and occasionally restructures during ingest.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6936:- **Why it matters for us**: This reduces the gap with Public. MemPalace is not pure “dump everything and search later”; it already depends on routing heuristics and ingest policy. That means Public can safely experiment with more verbatim evidence retention without abandoning its selective, governed save path.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6937:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6938:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6940:### Finding 4: The diary pattern is useful, but its current storage plane is architecturally messy
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6943:- **What it does**: The protocol says every session should end with `mempalace_diary_write`, and those diary entries are stored back into the same `mempalace_drawers` collection as searchable documents. The code itself admits raw AAAK degrades embedding quality but still stores it there.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6944:- **Why it matters for us**: The checkpoint habit is good; the storage choice is not. Public’s stronger model is to keep authoritative session capture in structured save contracts rather than blend compressed reflective text into the same main retrieval plane. If we adopt a diary/checkpoint feature, it should be a separate plane or explicitly down-weighted one.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6945:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6946:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6948:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s truthfulness upgrade is strongest in its docs, and Public should harden this in product surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6951:- **What it does**: On April 7, 2026, MemPalace publicly corrected overstated AAAK, palace-boost, contradiction, and rerank claims; the benchmark file explicitly labels hybrid v4 as test-set contamination; the protocol says “never guess.” But most of that honesty lives in docs and benchmark notes, not in runtime evidence-tiering.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6952:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6953:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6954:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7003:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7004:### Finding 1: MemPalace’s strongest pattern is lifecycle-enforced memory discipline, not just retrieval

End-to-end, the agent is told what memory is for, when to read it, and when it must preserve state.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7008:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the real differentiator. Public already has stronger save authority and recovery authority than MemPalace, via explicit-target JSON-primary saves, `session_bootstrap`, and compaction transport. What Public still lacks is the same enforced timing: a bounded preservation checkpoint before stop/compaction, plus one memory-facing bootstrap surface that teaches query/save discipline as directly as MemPalace does.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7009:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7017:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7023:- **What it does**: Project files are chunked into 800-character drawers with overlap and room routing; convo mode chunks by exchange pairs; “general” mode stops being raw filing and instead heuristically extracts five memory classes. So the system keeps verbatim text, but it still normalizes, routes, segments, and occasionally restructures during ingest.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7024:- **Why it matters for us**: This reduces the gap with Public. That means Public can safely experiment with more verbatim evidence retention without abandoning its selective, governed save path.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7025:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7026:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7028:### Finding 4: The diary pattern is useful, but its current storage plane is architecturally messy

The code itself admits raw AAAK degrades embedding quality but still stores it there.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7032:- **Why it matters for us**: The checkpoint habit is good; the storage choice is not. Public’s stronger model is to keep authoritative session capture in structured save contracts rather than blend compressed reflective text into the same main retrieval plane. If we adopt a diary/checkpoint feature, it should be a separate plane or explicitly down-weighted one.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7033:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7034:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7036:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s truthfulness upgrade is strongest in its docs, and Public should harden this in product surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7039:- **What it does**: On April 7, 2026, MemPalace publicly corrected overstated AAAK, palace-boost, contradiction, and rerank claims; the benchmark file explicitly labels hybrid v4 as test-set contamination; the protocol says “never guess.” But most of that honesty lives in docs and benchmark notes, not in runtime evidence-tiering.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7040:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7041:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7042:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:1:### Finding 5: The duplicate-check API is advisory, while write correctness is left to caller discipline
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:3:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest tool-contract weakness in MemPalace’s write surface. For Public, the takeaway is to keep duplicate/quality checks on the authoritative save path, not as optional caller behavior.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:6:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8:### Finding 6: MemPalace’s wake-up/search layers are real, but they are API-adjacent utilities rather than first-class MCP profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes L0/L1/L2/L3 through `layers.py` and the CLI, but the MCP layer does not expose those as distinct profiles or modes; it mostly exposes search, taxonomy, KG, and diary tools. Public’s `memory_context` and `memory_search` do expose explicit modes and profiles (`auto`, `deep`, `resume`, `research`, `debug`) directly in the API contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable piece is the conceptual layering, not the surface design. Public already has the stronger profile-separated API surface; MemPalace mainly reinforces that startup and retrieval modes should stay user-visible and tool-visible.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:71:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:142:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:143:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:144:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:155:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:156:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:157:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:254:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:256:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:258:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:259:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:260:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:261:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11877:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11879:### Finding 1: MemPalace does not have a first-class session model; it has hook-local session bookkeeping
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11881:- **What it does**: session state is just a sanitized `session_id`, a per-session `last_save` counter file under `~/.mempalace/hook_state`, and log lines; `session-start` only initializes the directory and returns `{}`. There is no persisted resume payload, interrupted-session recovery, or authoritative session record in the server.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11882:- **Why it matters for us**: this is much weaker than Public’s explicit recovery surfaces and server-managed continuity, which already expose `memory_context(...resume)`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, priming state, and crash recovery.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11883:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11884:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11886:### Finding 2: Save and pre-compact protection are real, but they are protocol prompts, not authoritative persistence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11888:- **What it does**: the stop hook blocks every 15 user messages and the precompact hook always blocks, but both only emit a “save now” reason. Optional auto-ingest merely runs `mempalace mine <dir>`; the source does not regenerate a critical-facts layer despite the README claim.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11889:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is useful, but MemPalace leaves correctness to agent obedience. Public’s JSON-primary `generate-context.js` save contract and compaction surfaces are stronger because the save path itself is authoritative.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11890:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11891:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11893:### Finding 3: Wake-up continuity is global importance-biased, not recent-session-aware, and it is not wired to the onboarding bootstrap files
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11895:- **What it does**: L0 reads only `~/.mempalace/identity.txt`, and L1 scans drawers, scores mostly by importance-like metadata, then formats the top 15 snippets. Despite the docstring mentioning “most-recent,” no recency sort is applied. Onboarding separately writes `aaak_entities.md` and `critical_facts.md`, but `wake_up()` never reads them.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11896:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s startup continuity is better described as “global memory boot text” than “resume my last session.” Public’s resume flow is already more explicit and session-shaped.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11897:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11898:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11900:### Finding 4: Conversation ingestion preserves searchable turns, but it strips session structure and compresses exchanges aggressively
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11902:- **What it does**: Codex JSONL normalization keeps only `session_meta` plus `event_msg` user/agent turns and skips `response_item` entries. Mining then chunks one user turn plus only the first eight assistant lines into a drawer, or falls back to paragraph groups.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11903:- **Why it matters for us**: this is useful as a raw-transcript import idea, but it is not faithful enough for exact session replay, tool-call reconstruction, or compaction recovery. Public’s JSON-primary save format is richer because it can carry tool calls, exchanges, preflight/postflight, and explicit session metadata.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11904:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11905:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11907:### Finding 5: The diary is MemPalace’s only explicit across-session summary lane, and it is agent-scoped rather than session-scoped
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11909:- **What it does**: the protocol tells the AI to call `mempalace_diary_write` after each session; entries are stored in `wing_<agent>/diary`, timestamped, then read back by `mempalace_diary_read`. The implementation explicitly notes that raw AAAK is stored even though compressed AAAK degrades embedding quality.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11910:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the strongest reusable lifecycle idea in MemPalace: a separate “agent journal” lane for cross-session continuity. But it should not replace Public’s main resume/save path; it would be additive.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11911:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11912:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11914:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger lifecycle architecture; MemPalace mainly contributes selective ideas, not a better overall model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11916:- **What it does**: Public has explicit session recovery APIs, per-session priming keyed by `sessionId`, interrupted-session crash recovery, compaction-time auto-surfacing, and a JSON-primary save contract that treats the live session as the source of truth.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11917:- **Why it matters for us**: the right takeaway is narrow: consider a raw transcript import lane and maybe an agent journal lane. Do not copy MemPalace’s lifecycle surface wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11918:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11919:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11968:## Findings

But it should not replace Public’s main resume/save path; it would be additive.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12002:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
