.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12008:- **Why it matters for us**: the right takeaway is narrow: consider a raw transcript import lane and maybe an agent journal lane. Do not copy MemPalace’s lifecycle surface wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12009:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12010:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:1:### Finding 6: “store everything” is still bounded by silent skips, lossy reads, and heuristic side paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:3:- **What it does**: project mining reads text with `errors="replace"`, silently skips unreadable or oversized files, and deletes stale drawers before re-inserting. Conversation mining skips normalization failures and, in `general` mode, routes chunks through a heuristic extractor that classifies decisions/problems/emotions instead of keeping the original exchange as the only authoritative unit.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: the real architecture is “keep a lot, but only if it is readable and routeable,” not literal total preservation. Public’s JSON-primary session capture is actually stricter about source authority than MemPalace’s heuristically restructured side paths.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:88:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:159:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:160:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:161:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:172:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:173:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:174:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:271:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:273:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:275:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:276:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:277:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:278:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5325:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5327:### Finding 1: Hook-enforced pre-compaction preservation is the strongest transferable pattern
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5329:- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on two timing edges: after enough user turns it emits a save instruction, and before compaction it always blocks and can optionally run synchronous ingest before context shrinks. Public already has a compaction hook surface, but it currently injects structural transport context rather than triggering a governed memory save.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5330:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest integration candidate because Public already has the compaction interception point and already prefers JSON-primary saves. Integration difficulty is **medium**: the hook surface exists, but we would need a strict contract so compaction can only trigger structured `generate-context.js` style evidence capture, not MemPalace’s free-form “save everything” prompt.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5331:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5332:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5334:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol bootstrap is the easiest thing to port safely
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5336:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` does more than health reporting: it returns the palace overview plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly teaching the agent when to query, when to write diaries, and when to invalidate facts. Public already emits recovery and routing guidance in `context-server.ts`, but not a short memory-discipline protocol this direct.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5337:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the right bootstrap surfaces, so this is a **low** integration lift. A small “memory operating protocol” added to `session_bootstrap` or an equivalent status surface would reinforce verify-before-answer and recovery order without importing MemPalace’s heavier framing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5338:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5339:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5341:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 stack is reusable only as a thin digest, not as a full model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5343:- **What it does**: In code, L0 is a static `~/.mempalace/identity.txt`, L1 is a capped summary generated from the top 15 drawers by simple importance sorting, L2 is filtered `get`, and L3 is the same Chroma semantic search. The implementation says wake-up is roughly 600-900 tokens, not the much lighter story implied in the README table.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5344:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context(resume)`, and `memory_match_triggers`, so a full port would duplicate stronger existing recovery paths with a weaker ranking heuristic. Integration difficulty is **medium-high**. The reusable part is only a thin, project-scoped startup digest layer, not the entire MemPalace layering model.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5345:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5346:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5348:### Finding 4: Full palace taxonomy is expensive to integrate and not differentiated enough
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5350:- **What it does**: MemPalace room assignment is mostly folder-name and keyword heuristics at ingest, plain Chroma metadata filters at retrieval, and a derived graph built from shared `wing`/`room` metadata. The README’s own correction note says the “+34% palace boost” is just metadata filtering, and the benchmark notes show room routing still trails the no-rooms hybrid baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5351:- **Why it matters for us**: Public would need new save-time taxonomy generation, label governance, reindex logic, and drift controls just to recreate a layer that is mostly packaging plus filters. Integration difficulty is **high**, and the unique upside is too small given Public already has spec-folder scoping, context types, tiers, and structural code graph tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5352:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5353:- **Impact**: low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5355:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5357:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps a separate SQLite fact store with `entities`, `triples`, `valid_from`, and `valid_to`, plus dedicated add/query/invalidate/timeline operations. That separation is real and useful: it prevents temporal facts from being conflated with raw drawer retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5358:- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently has strong recall/search and causal links, but not a fact plane with temporal invalidation semantics. Integration difficulty is **high** because this would need governed fact extraction, citation requirements, correction workflows, and clear authority boundaries between facts and verbatim memories. The architecture is worth exploring, but only as a stricter Public-native subsystem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5359:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5360:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5409:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5411:### Finding 1: Hook-enforced pre-compaction preservation is the strongest transferable pattern
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5413:- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on two timing edges: after enough user turns it emits a save instruction, and before compaction it always blocks and can optionally run synchronous ingest before context shrinks. Public already has a compaction hook surface, but it currently injects structural transport context rather than triggering a governed memory save.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5414:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest integration candidate because Public already has the compaction interception point and already prefers JSON-primary saves. Integration difficulty is **medium**: the hook surface exists, but we would need a strict contract so compaction can only trigger structured `generate-context.js` style evidence capture, not MemPalace’s free-form “save everything” prompt.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5415:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5420:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` does more than health reporting: it returns the palace overview plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly teaching the agent when to query, when to write diaries, and when to invalidate facts. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5421:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the right bootstrap surfaces, so this is a **low** integration lift. The implementation says wake-up is roughly 600-900 tokens, not the much lighter story implied in the README table.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5429:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

Integration difficulty is **high**, and the unique upside is too small given Public already has spec-folder scoping, context types, tiers, and structural code graph tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5436:- **Recommendation**: reject

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5439:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5441:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps a separate SQLite fact store with `entities`, `triples`, `valid_from`, and `valid_to`, plus dedicated add/query/invalidate/timeline operations. That separation is real and useful: it prevents temporal facts from being conflated with raw drawer retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5442:- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently has strong recall/search and causal links, but not a fact plane with temporal invalidation semantics. The architecture is worth exploring, but only as a stricter Public-native subsystem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5443:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5444:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:1:### Finding 5: Deletion and garbage collection are hard-delete maintenance paths with audit hints, not soft-delete lifecycle controls
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:3:- **What it does**: Source-confirmed. Writes are logged to a JSONL WAL before execution, but drawer removal is still a real `col.delete()`, dedup physically deletes near-duplicates, and `repair.py` scans, prunes corrupt IDs, or fully rebuilds the collection after extracting all drawers.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace has useful operational repair instincts, especially around vector-store corruption, but not a soft-delete or retention model. For us, the reusable part is repair tooling and pre-rebuild backup discipline, not the delete semantics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger hygiene architecture: append-only lineage, archival supersession, immutable audit, and logical-key repair
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10:- **What it does**: Source-confirmed. Public reconsolidates on save, archives superseded rows instead of overwriting them, records `logical_key`, `version_number`, `valid_to`, and `superseded_by_memory_id`, repairs active projections when logical identity drifts, and keeps an append-only `mutation_ledger` guarded by triggers against update/delete.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: On memory hygiene specifically, Public is already beyond MemPalace. We should not import MemPalace’s drawer lifecycle wholesale; we should only cherry-pick targeted ideas like offline corruption repair or a canonical topic-key layer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:12:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:13:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:90:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:161:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:162:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:163:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:174:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:175:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:176:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:273:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:275:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:277:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:278:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:279:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:280:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10560:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10561:### Finding 1: `mempalace_status` is a behavior bootstrap, not just a health check
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10563:- **What it does**: `tool_status()` returns drawer counts plus two embedded instruction payloads: `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`. The protocol explicitly tells the agent to call status on wake-up, query before answering, write a diary after each session, and invalidate stale KG facts before adding new ones. This makes the first memory call double as an operational contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10564:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest MemPalace agent-integration idea in the codebase. It reduces dependency on separate prompt docs because the memory surface itself teaches the right behavior. Public already has stronger startup guidance in MCP startup instructions and `session_bootstrap`, but it does not currently make one memory-facing tool return such a tight “how to use memory safely” contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10565:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10566:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10568:### Finding 2: Passive capture is genuinely agent-agnostic in concept, but classification safety is delegated back to the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10570:- **What it does**: MemPalace implements hooks twice: shell hooks for Claude/Codex install docs, and a Python `hooks_cli.py` layer that already abstracts `session-start`, `stop`, and `precompact` for multiple harnesses. The stop hook counts user messages, blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL`, and emits a save instruction. The precompact hook always blocks and tells the agent to save everything before context loss. Optional `MEMPAL_DIR` auto-ingest exists, but the normal path tells the agent to classify and file the session itself.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10571:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable value is not the exact shell scripts; it is the timing contract. MemPalace proves that passive capture can sit outside one model vendor and work as a harness-level preservation mechanism. The weakness is that once blocked, safety depends on the agent correctly choosing categories and scope with no governed boundaries.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10572:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10573:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10575:### Finding 3: “Specialist agents” are implemented as separate diary wings, not as a real multi-agent safety model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10577:- **What it does**: The real source-backed multi-agent feature is `mempalace_diary_write/read`, which stores each agent’s journal under `wing_<agent>/room=diary`. There is no source-backed `mempalace_list_agents` tool, no agent registry in the MCP tool table, and no enforcement that an agent reads only its own diary or avoids other wings.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10578:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a useful reminder that per-agent memory can start as simple namespaced journals, but it is not enough for multi-agent safety. MemPalace’s README sells stronger agent specialization than the implementation currently proves. For Public, this means we should treat diary-per-agent as a lightweight UX idea, not as an isolation or trust boundary.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10579:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10580:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10582:### Finding 4: Project scoping is ergonomic metadata routing, not governed scope control
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10584:- **What it does**: Scoping is mostly `wing` and `room` metadata. Search, L2 recall, and L3 search all just build `where` filters on those fields. Conversation mining defaults the wing from the folder name if none is passed. Project mining routes files into rooms via folder/filename/keyword heuristics, and on re-mine it deletes all existing drawers for a source file before re-inserting fresh chunks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10585:- **Why it matters for us**: This is good human-facing narrowing, but it is not safe scope governance. There is no tenant/user/agent/shared-space model, no cursor scoping, and no protected boundary between “my project” and “your project” beyond labels. The upside is ergonomic: the wing/room vocabulary is memorable and agent-friendly. The downside is that it is easy to mis-file or over-share unless another layer enforces scope.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10586:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10587:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10589:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10591:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10592:- **Why it matters for us**: On agent integration and project safety, Public is ahead. MemPalace contributes a cleaner, more memorable behavioral surface, but not a safer one. The transferable parts are the small ones: one explicit memory protocol surface, and harness-level passive save timing. The scoping and multi-agent model should not be imported wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10593:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10594:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10643:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10644:### Finding 1: `mempalace_status` is a behavior bootstrap, not just a health check
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10646:- **What it does**: `tool_status()` returns drawer counts plus two embedded instruction payloads: `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`. The protocol explicitly tells the agent to call status on wake-up, query before answering, write a diary after each session, and invalidate stale KG facts before adding new ones. This makes the first memory call double as an operational contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10647:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest MemPalace agent-integration idea in the codebase. It reduces dependency on separate prompt docs because the memory surface itself teaches the right behavior. Public already has stronger startup guidance in MCP startup instructions and `session_bootstrap`, but it does not currently make one memory-facing tool return such a tight “how to use memory safely” contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10648:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10649:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10651:### Finding 2: Passive capture is genuinely agent-agnostic in concept, but classification safety is delegated back to the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10653:- **What it does**: MemPalace implements hooks twice: shell hooks for Claude/Codex install docs, and a Python `hooks_cli.py` layer that already abstracts `session-start`, `stop`, and `precompact` for multiple harnesses. The stop hook counts user messages, blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL`, and emits a save instruction. The precompact hook always blocks and tells the agent to save everything before context loss. Optional `MEMPAL_DIR` auto-ingest exists, but the normal path tells the agent to classify and file the session itself.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10654:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable value is not the exact shell scripts; it is the timing contract. MemPalace proves that passive capture can sit outside one model vendor and work as a harness-level preservation mechanism. The weakness is that once blocked, safety depends on the agent correctly choosing categories and scope with no governed boundaries.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10655:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10656:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10658:### Finding 3: “Specialist agents” are implemented as separate diary wings, not as a real multi-agent safety model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10660:- **What it does**: The real source-backed multi-agent feature is `mempalace_diary_write/read`, which stores each agent’s journal under `wing_<agent>/room=diary`. There is no source-backed `mempalace_list_agents` tool, no agent registry in the MCP tool table, and no enforcement that an agent reads only its own diary or avoids other wings.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10661:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a useful reminder that per-agent memory can start as simple namespaced journals, but it is not enough for multi-agent safety. MemPalace’s README sells stronger agent specialization than the implementation currently proves. For Public, this means we should treat diary-per-agent as a lightweight UX idea, not as an isolation or trust boundary.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10662:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10663:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10665:### Finding 4: Project scoping is ergonomic metadata routing, not governed scope control
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10667:- **What it does**: Scoping is mostly `wing` and `room` metadata. Search, L2 recall, and L3 search all just build `where` filters on those fields. Conversation mining defaults the wing from the folder name if none is passed. Project mining routes files into rooms via folder/filename/keyword heuristics, and on re-mine it deletes all existing drawers for a source file before re-inserting fresh chunks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10668:- **Why it matters for us**: This is good human-facing narrowing, but it is not safe scope governance. There is no tenant/user/agent/shared-space model, no cursor scoping, and no protected boundary between “my project” and “your project” beyond labels. The upside is ergonomic: the wing/room vocabulary is memorable and agent-friendly. The downside is that it is easy to mis-file or over-share unless another layer enforces scope.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10669:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10670:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10672:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10674:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10675:- **Why it matters for us**: On agent integration and project safety, Public is ahead. MemPalace contributes a cleaner, more memorable behavioral surface, but not a safer one. The transferable parts are the small ones: one explicit memory protocol surface, and harness-level passive save timing. The scoping and multi-agent model should not be imported wholesale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10676:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10677:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:1:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:3:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s layer stack is conceptually neat, but it duplicates search/filter logic already present in `searcher.py`, emits formatted text rather than one authoritative structured result shape, and still uses rough token heuristics (`len(...) // 4`). Public already has profile-aware orchestration and budget enforcement in `memory_context`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: For Public, a separate wake-up engine would duplicate ranking, result formatting, and token-budget logic we already solved elsewhere. If we want L0/L1-like behavior, it should be expressed as an existing `memory_context`/`session_bootstrap` profile, not as a parallel retrieval subsystem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8:### Finding 5: Public should preserve its modular handler architecture and not absorb new memory features into a single giant server file
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s `mcp_server.py` owns tool registration, bootstrap text, drawer writes, KG writes, diary writes, and JSON-RPC dispatch in one file. Public splits orchestration, retrieval, save validation, and server lifecycle into dedicated modules.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: The next wave of ideas here is taxonomy, hooks, and maybe identity UX. A monolithic merge would make write authority, governance, and review harder. The safer direction is to add any new capabilities as bounded handlers layered on top of the existing validated save/search contracts.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:12:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:88:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:159:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:160:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:161:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:172:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:173:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:174:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:271:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:273:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:275:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:276:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:277:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:278:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6004:   176	**What it does:** Stores verbatim session text. Embeds with sentence transformers. Retrieves by cosine similarity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6095:     3	- **What it does**: MemPalace has a separate SQLite triple store with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, manual invalidation, and `source_closet` provenance, but its own README says contradiction wiring is still pending. Public’s causal layer is less fact-like, yet it validates relation types, clamps strengths to finite `0..1`, caps auto-edge strength, clears graph caches on mutation, cleans orphaned edges, and reports graph coverage.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6096:     4	- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace wins on fact expressiveness, Public wins on operational hygiene. The reusable idea is temporal validity on edges, not the current MemPalace fact pipeline as-is.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6097:     5	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6098:     6	- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6102:    10	- **What it does**: Conversation ingest truncates AI responses to the first 8 lines, room assignment is keyword-scored, heuristic extraction only keeps paragraphs that clear a confidence floor, and project re-mines delete and reinsert prior drawers. That is not “unfiltered raw memory”; it is selective routing and reduction. Public is also selective, but it makes that explicit through trigger filtering and save quality controls instead of presenting the result as untouched raw capture.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6103:    11	- **Why it matters for us**: Hygiene depends on truthfulness about loss and transformation. MemPalace’s real pipeline is workable, but its storage story is cleaner in prose than in code.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6104:    12	- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6105:    13	- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6109:    17	- **What it does**: MemPalace’s diary tool explicitly notes that raw AAAK is stored even though compressed AAAK degrades embedding quality. Public has no direct equivalent diary feature, but its save path already expects validation and quality gating before long-term indexing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6110:    18	- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever add agent diaries, this is the exact hygiene pitfall to avoid: compressed recall artifacts should not become the primary indexed representation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6111:    19	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6112:    20	- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6172:    80	What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6243:   151	- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6244:   152	- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6245:   153	- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6256:   164	- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6257:   165	- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:6258:   166	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9734:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9735:### Finding 1: Memory becomes a lifecycle policy, not just a retrieval API
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9737:- **What it does**: MemPalace treats memory as a timed operating discipline: `mempalace_status` teaches query/save behavior up front, the Stop hook blocks every 15 user turns, and PreCompact always blocks before loss. Public already primes sessions, auto-surfaces context, and injects compaction transport, but it mostly nudges rather than enforces preservation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9738:- **Why it matters for us**: The deeper shift is that “memory architecture” includes lifecycle checkpoints and agent behavior contracts, not just storage and ranking. Public should think in terms of preservation policy surfaces, especially around compaction and long sessions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9739:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9740:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9742:### Finding 2: Context delivery should be a response compiler, not a separate wake-up stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9744:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s L0/L1/L2/L3 model is mostly formatted text assembly with rough `len(...) // 4` token estimates and separate rendering paths. Public already does something more powerful: mode routing, structured envelopes, profile selection, progressive disclosure, session dedup, and actual budget enforcement with truncation and metadata-only spillover.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9745:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is to treat memory output as a compiled artifact shaped for task, pressure, and budget, not as a hardcoded layer stack. That makes wake-up/resume a profile problem, not a separate engine problem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9746:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9747:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9749:### Finding 3: A strong memory system can have multiple planes of truth without forcing them into one index
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9751:- **What it does**: MemPalace separates three representational planes: verbatim drawers in ChromaDB, a navigational graph derived from room metadata, and a separate temporal triple store with invalidation. Public already hints at the same architectural direction by keeping semantic memory, code-graph structure, and orchestration distinct.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9752:- **Why it matters for us**: The big shift is representational pluralism. If we need fact-time validity, it should likely live in its own bounded fact plane rather than being forced into causal links, search metadata, or the main memory row shape.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9753:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9754:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9756:### Finding 4: Authority surfaces matter more than feature count
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9758:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes many overlapping write authorities: direct drawer writes, diary writes, KG writes, AI-directed hook saves, and optional hook auto-ingest. Public is stricter: JSON-primary save is the canonical path, explicit target spec folder wins, direct spec-folder mode is rejected, and session recovery authority is explicitly preserved by `session_bootstrap`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9759:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is to architect memory around a few clear authority owners: who decides where memory is saved, who owns session recovery truth, who injects compaction transport, and who is allowed to write derived fact state. That is a better scaling principle than adding more tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9760:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9761:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9763:### Finding 5: Identity should be governed internally and human-friendly externally
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9765:- **What it does**: MemPalace makes people/projects/wings a first-class user-facing taxonomy from the start. Public instead grounds retrieval in governed scope fields like `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9766:- **Why it matters for us**: The shift is not “copy wings.” It is to split identity into two planes: machine-trustworthy internal scope and optional human-friendly aliases/navigation. That would give us MemPalace’s ergonomic browsing without weakening governed retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9767:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9768:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9770:### Finding 6: Truthfulness boundaries are part of the architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9772:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s own correction note shows how easily mode boundaries blur: raw mode, rooms mode, AAAK, and unreleased rerank paths were marketed too closely together. Public already encodes stricter guardrails in structured save contracts and confidence floors.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9773:- **Why it matters for us**: A memory system is not trustworthy if its interfaces blur what is stored, what is derived, what is experimental, and what is benchmark-only. Evidence tiering needs to be part of the product contract, not just a doc habit.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9774:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9775:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9811:## Findings

That is a better scaling principle than adding more tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9837:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
