.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9842:- **What it does**: MemPalace makes people/projects/wings a first-class user-facing taxonomy from the start. Public instead grounds retrieval in governed scope fields like `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9843:- **Why it matters for us**: The shift is not “copy wings.” It is to split identity into two planes: machine-trustworthy internal scope and optional human-friendly aliases/navigation. That would give us MemPalace’s ergonomic browsing without weakening governed retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9844:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9845:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9847:### Finding 6: Truthfulness boundaries are part of the architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9849:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s own correction note shows how easily mode boundaries blur: raw mode, rooms mode, AAAK, and unreleased rerank paths were marketed too closely together. Public already encodes stricter guardrails in structured save contracts and confidence floors.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9850:- **Why it matters for us**: A memory system is not trustworthy if its interfaces blur what is stored, what is derived, what is experimental, and what is benchmark-only. Evidence tiering needs to be part of the product contract, not just a doc habit.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9851:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9852:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:1:### Finding 4: Public already has a partial analogue, but it stops at memory lineage rather than first-class facts
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:4:- **What it does**: Public has causal relations, optional temporal columns on those edges, and correction-driven `supersedes` / `derived_from` linkage. But the exposed tool surface is still lineage-oriented: `memory_drift_why`, `memory_causal_link`, `memory_causal_stats`, `memory_causal_unlink`. There is no entity/predicate/object fact query, no `as_of`, and no fact timeline tool.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:5:- **Why it matters for us**: The gap is narrower than it looks. Public does not need a wholesale graph rewrite; it needs a separate fact model and a user-facing query layer if we want MemPalace-style temporal recall.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:6:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:7:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:9:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s fact plane is useful, but its provenance and hygiene are too loose for direct adoption
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:12:- **What it does**: MemPalace normalizes entities with simple string munging, allows fact insertion with optional provenance fields, and relies on protocol discipline rather than hard source contracts. Meanwhile its diary still writes compressed reflective text into the main drawer collection. Public’s JSON-primary save path is stricter about authoritative session capture and scope.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:13:- **Why it matters for us**: The right transfer is the plane separation, not the loose write path. If Public adopts temporal facts, they should be derived from structured save evidence or explicit approved mutations, not free-form manual assertions mixed with diary-style material.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:14:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:15:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:### Finding 6: Palace graph and temporal KG are intentionally different systems, and Public should keep that separation too
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:20:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s palace graph is derived from Chroma metadata like shared rooms and wings; the KG is explicit temporal triples. Public has a similar architectural instinct already: structural bootstrap and routing are separate from memory tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:21:- **Why it matters for us**: We should not collapse code graph, causal graph, and any future fact graph into one “graph feature.” The cleaner design is separate planes with clear query intents.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:22:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:23:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:95:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:166:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:167:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:168:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:179:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:180:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:181:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:278:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:280:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:282:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:283:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:284:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:285:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11164:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11165:### Finding 1: `mempalace_add_drawer` can silently alias distinct memories instead of truly deduplicating them
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11167:- **What it does**: there is a separate semantic duplicate checker, but `tool_add_drawer` does not call it; instead it derives `drawer_id` from `wing + room + content[:100]` and treats an existing ID as `"already_exists"`. Two different long entries with the same first 100 chars in the same wing/room collapse to one record.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11168:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the worst kind of failure mode for memory: silent false idempotency. Public’s JSON-primary save path is stricter about explicit inputs and validation; if we ever add free-form fact writes, we should not key them off truncated content prefixes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11169:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11170:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11172:### Finding 2: migration and repair are first-class concepts, but the live recovery path is still heavy and partially fragmented
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11174:- **What it does**: MemPalace has explicit `migrate`, `repair`, `scan`, `prune`, and rebuild flows, but they still extract large portions of the palace into memory. `migrate()` swaps the fresh palace into place before checking whether `final_count` matched expectation, and the simpler CLI `repair` path is still a full collection rebuild, while the more surgical `repair.py` flow is not the main CLI surface.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11175:- **Why it matters for us**: the lesson is to keep recovery tooling explicit and separate from normal retrieval, but pair it with staged verification and pre-swap integrity checks. Public already leans this way with structured validation, recovery hints, and bridge isolation instead of mutating the live runtime blindly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11176:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11177:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11179:### Finding 3: health and taxonomy surfaces prefer “return something” over “return the whole truth”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11181:- **What it does**: `status`, `list_wings`, `list_rooms`, and `taxonomy` swallow exceptions and cap metadata scans at `limit=10000`, so large palaces can undercount silently. Separately, `palace_graph.build_graph()` still walks the full collection in batches. The benchmark suite still describes older scaling assumptions like unbounded `tool_status()` and client re-instantiation, which no longer match the live MCP code.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11182:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s own failure story shows why status tools need coverage metadata and truthfulness guards. Public is already closer to the right pattern: `session_bootstrap`, `context-server`, and `memory_search` expose stale warnings, recovery hints, and bounded fallback behavior instead of silent partial truth.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11183:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11184:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11186:### Finding 4: hook-driven preservation is useful, but its timeout and parsing envelope is brittle
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11188:- **What it does**: the hooks correctly force save checkpoints, but failure handling is uneven. Malformed stdin degrades to empty data and `"unknown"` session state; Python precompact only catches `OSError`, so a timed-out synchronous auto-ingest can bubble out before emitting the block JSON; the shell precompact path runs auto-ingest synchronously with no timeout at all.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11189:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is strong, but the implementation shows that save enforcement must be bounded and structured. Public’s compaction/recovery surfaces are already safer because `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and explicit, and the compact-code-graph bridge fails closed on timeout instead of hanging the host.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11190:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11191:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11193:### Finding 5: the temporal KG is operational, but truth maintenance under concurrency is weak
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11195:- **What it does**: MemPalace uses one SQLite connection with `check_same_thread=False`, no retry/backoff layer, and benchmark code explicitly measures concurrent read/write failures. `invalidate()` does not report whether anything was actually closed, and `timeline()` is a full ordered scan capped to 100 rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11196:- **Why it matters for us**: this reinforces the earlier conclusion that Public should keep any future fact plane separate from recall/search, but also governed and auditable. Our current lineage/correction model is slower to evolve, but it is less likely to claim a fact update succeeded when nothing changed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11197:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11198:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11200:### Finding 6: “store everything” is still bounded by silent skips, lossy reads, and heuristic side paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11202:- **What it does**: project mining reads text with `errors="replace"`, silently skips unreadable or oversized files, and deletes stale drawers before re-inserting. Conversation mining skips normalization failures and, in `general` mode, routes chunks through a heuristic extractor that classifies decisions/problems/emotions instead of keeping the original exchange as the only authoritative unit.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11203:- **Why it matters for us**: the real architecture is “keep a lot, but only if it is readable and routeable,” not literal total preservation. Public’s JSON-primary session capture is actually stricter about source authority than MemPalace’s heuristically restructured side paths.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11204:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11205:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11262:## Findings

Our current lineage/correction model is slower to evolve, but it is less likely to claim a fact update succeeded when nothing changed.

Conversation mining skips normalization failures and, in `general` mode, routes chunks through a heuristic extractor that classifies decisions/problems/emotions instead of keeping the original exchange as the only authoritative unit.

Public’s JSON-primary session capture is actually stricter about source authority than MemPalace’s heuristically restructured side paths.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11302:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11303:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:1:### Finding 4: Full palace taxonomy is expensive to integrate and not differentiated enough
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:3:- **What it does**: MemPalace room assignment is mostly folder-name and keyword heuristics at ingest, plain Chroma metadata filters at retrieval, and a derived graph built from shared `wing`/`room` metadata. The README’s own correction note says the “+34% palace boost” is just metadata filtering, and the benchmark notes show room routing still trails the no-rooms hybrid baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: Public would need new save-time taxonomy generation, label governance, reindex logic, and drift controls just to recreate a layer that is mostly packaging plus filters. Integration difficulty is **high**, and the unique upside is too small given Public already has spec-folder scoping, context types, tiers, and structural code graph tools.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:6:- **Impact**: low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:8:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps a separate SQLite fact store with `entities`, `triples`, `valid_from`, and `valid_to`, plus dedicated add/query/invalidate/timeline operations. That separation is real and useful: it prevents temporal facts from being conflated with raw drawer retrieval.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently has strong recall/search and causal links, but not a fact plane with temporal invalidation semantics. Integration difficulty is **high** because this would need governed fact extraction, citation requirements, correction workflows, and clear authority boundaries between facts and verbatim memories. The architecture is worth exploring, but only as a stricter Public-native subsystem.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:12:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:13:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:87:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:158:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:159:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:160:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:171:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:172:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:173:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:270:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:272:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:274:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:275:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:276:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:277:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7097:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7098:### Finding 1: Hook-driven preservation can create blocking saves without proof that good data was actually preserved
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7100:- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks every 15 user turns and always blocks before compaction, then tells the AI to save; optional auto-ingest is backgrounded in the stop hook and synchronous only if `MEMPAL_DIR` is configured. The write path itself is weakly protected: `mempalace_add_drawer` uses deterministic IDs, but it does not actually call the semantic duplicate checker before writing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7101:- **Why it matters for us**: If we copy this pattern into Public, the biggest failure mode is a false sense of safety: the runtime is interrupted, but preservation quality still depends on the model following instructions correctly. That can yield duplicated memories, noisy categorization, partial saves, or blocked UX with no durable improvement. Public’s current compaction transport and JSON-primary `generate-context.js` path are much more deterministic.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7102:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7103:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7105:### Finding 2: A thin wake-up digest is reusable, but MemPalace’s actual L1 ranking is too naive to trust as a primary bootstrap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7107:- **What it does**: L0 is a static `identity.txt`; L1 loads every drawer, scores by a few metadata keys, sorts by importance only, takes the top 15, groups by room, and truncates snippets to fit a char cap. Despite comments about “most-recent,” there is no real recency or authority model in the ranking.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7108:- **Why it matters for us**: A Public-native startup digest could still be useful, but adopting MemPalace’s ranking logic directly would privilege stale but highly weighted memories, flatten provenance, and compete with stronger existing recovery paths like `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context(resume)`. The risk is not just mediocre recall; it is confidently front-loading the wrong context.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7109:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7110:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7112:### Finding 3: Status-as-protocol-bootstrap is the lowest-risk transfer, but only if we keep it much smaller than MemPalace does
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7114:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns palace counts plus a full `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly teaching the agent when to query, when to diary-write, and when to invalidate facts. Public already has startup guidance, recovery hints, and tool routing, but its own code explicitly notes those instructions are computed once at startup and not refreshed mid-session.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7115:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable idea is strong: one surface can teach memory behavior, not just expose health. The risk is that a MemPalace-sized protocol blob would become stale, conflict with Public’s existing bootstrap authority, and add prompt noise faster than it adds reliability. The safe version is a short, stable rule set, not a second operating manual.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7116:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7117:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7119:### Finding 4: A separate temporal fact plane is high-upside, but MemPalace’s current source contract is too loose for Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7121:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, separate from Chroma drawers. That split is real. But `kg_add` does not require source evidence, contradiction detection is not actually wired into KG ops, and `invalidate` updates all matching live triples with no stronger review step.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7122:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the one new architecture that Public genuinely lacks. The risk is governance, not mechanics: uncited fact insertion, ambiguous invalidation, silent drift between fact rows and verbatim memory, and agent-written “truth” that outruns its evidence. If we build this, it needs mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction workflows, and separation from ordinary memory writes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7123:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7124:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7126:### Finding 5: Full palace taxonomy remains the easiest pattern to overpay for and the hardest to keep honest
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7128:- **What it does**: Room assignment is driven by folder-name maps, filename/path matches, and keyword counts; the palace graph is then derived from shared `wing`/`room`/`hall` metadata. The repo’s own correction note says the famous “palace boost” was mostly metadata filtering, and benchmark notes show roomed routing still trailing the stronger hybrid no-rooms baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7129:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public adopted this whole taxonomy, we would be buying label governance, reindex churn, room drift, fuzzy routing mistakes, and operator overhead for a capability that mostly repackages filtering we already have via scope, context types, and graph/search tools. The main thing that could go wrong is spending real product complexity on a memorable metaphor that does not improve truthfulness or recovery enough to justify itself.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7130:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7131:- **Impact**: low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7179:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7180:### Finding 1: Hook-driven preservation can create blocking saves without proof that good data was actually preserved

The write path itself is weakly protected: `mempalace_add_drawer` uses deterministic IDs, but it does not actually call the semantic duplicate checker before writing.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7190:- **Why it matters for us**: A Public-native startup digest could still be useful, but adopting MemPalace’s ranking logic directly would privilege stale but highly weighted memories, flatten provenance, and compete with stronger existing recovery paths like `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context(resume)`. The risk is not just mediocre recall; it is confidently front-loading the wrong context.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7191:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7192:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7194:### Finding 3: Status-as-protocol-bootstrap is the lowest-risk transfer, but only if we keep it much smaller than MemPalace does
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7196:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns palace counts plus a full `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly teaching the agent when to query, when to diary-write, and when to invalidate facts.
