The risk is that a MemPalace-sized protocol blob would become stale, conflict with Public’s existing bootstrap authority, and add prompt noise faster than it adds reliability. The safe version is a short, stable rule set, not a second operating manual.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7198:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7199:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7201:### Finding 4: A separate temporal fact plane is high-upside, but MemPalace’s current source contract is too loose for Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7203:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, separate from Chroma drawers. That split is real. But `kg_add` does not require source evidence, contradiction detection is not actually wired into KG ops, and `invalidate` updates all matching live triples with no stronger review step.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7204:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the one new architecture that Public genuinely lacks. The risk is governance, not mechanics: uncited fact insertion, ambiguous invalidation, silent drift between fact rows and verbatim memory, and agent-written “truth” that outruns its evidence. If we build this, it needs mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction workflows, and separation from ordinary memory writes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7205:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7206:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7208:### Finding 5: Full palace taxonomy remains the easiest pattern to overpay for and the hardest to keep honest
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7210:- **What it does**: Room assignment is driven by folder-name maps, filename/path matches, and keyword counts; the palace graph is then derived from shared `wing`/`room`/`hall` metadata. The repo’s own correction note says the famous “palace boost” was mostly metadata filtering, and benchmark notes show roomed routing still trailing the stronger hybrid no-rooms baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7211:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public adopted this whole taxonomy, we would be buying label governance, reindex churn, room drift, fuzzy routing mistakes, and operator overhead for a capability that mostly repackages filtering we already have via scope, context types, and graph/search tools. The main thing that could go wrong is spending real product complexity on a memorable metaphor that does not improve truthfulness or recovery enough to justify itself.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7212:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7213:- **Impact**: low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:1:### Finding 6: Recovery design treats `chroma.sqlite3` as the durable source-of-truth and HNSW as disposable
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:4:- **What it does**: `migrate.py` reads Chroma’s SQLite tables directly across version mismatches, reconstructs drawer documents and metadata, and imports them into a fresh collection. `repair.py` backs up only `chroma.sqlite3`, scans corrupt IDs, then rebuilds the HNSW index with `hnsw:space=cosine`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:5:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest storage-layer lesson in the repo. Public already rebuilds derived indexes and forces WAL, but the MemPalace posture of “logical DB first, vector index rebuildable” is worth carrying forward anywhere our vector layer can drift or corrupt.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9:### Finding 7: Public already has the stronger normalized storage model; MemPalace’s reusable value is a raw-verbatim sidecar, not a backend replacement
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12:- **What it does**: Public persists a normalized `memory_index`, FTS5 mirror, chunk parent/child rows, active lineage projection, causal edges, BM25 rebuild, and enforced WAL mode. MemPalace persists a simpler raw drawer collection plus sidecar stores.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:13:- **Why it matters for us**: Replacing Public’s backend with MemPalace would be a regression. The right move is a new raw-verbatim capture/archive lane that feeds or coexists with Public’s richer index.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:14:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:75:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:146:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:147:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:148:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:159:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:160:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:161:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:258:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:260:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:262:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:263:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:264:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:265:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8408:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8409:### Finding 1: The real CLI surface is broader than the README, and `init` does not execute the guided onboarding path it advertises
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8411:- **What it does**: The documented CLI presents `init` as guided onboarding plus AAAK bootstrap, but `cmd_init()` only runs entity scan, room detection, and config init; the actual interactive onboarding flow lives separately in `run_onboarding()` and is never called by the CLI entrypoint. The shipped CLI also includes undocumented operational subcommands like `hook run` and `instructions`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8412:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a source-confirmed drift between product story and real command surface. For Public, the reusable lesson is not “copy the README UX,” but “keep bootstrap commands honest and keep operational subcommands explicit.”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8413:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8414:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8416:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s MCP server is a thin stdin/stdout JSON-RPC transport with a single flat tool registry
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8418:- **What it does**: MemPalace negotiates `initialize`, serves `tools/list`, and dispatches `tools/call` from one `TOOLS` dict of 19 entries, coercing basic numeric types and returning each result as JSON text. Logs go to `stderr`, protocol payloads go to `stdout`, which is handshake-clean. By contrast, Public’s current MCP surface is schema-driven and much broader, with dozens of typed tool definitions and centralized dispatch.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8419:- **Why it matters for us**: The good idea here is transport simplicity and stderr discipline. The limitation is that capability separation, response shaping, and policy routing all live outside the protocol layer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8420:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8421:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8423:### Finding 3: `mempalace_status` is not just health output; it is an embedded operating protocol for the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8425:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns drawer counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to call search/KG tools before answering, invalidate stale facts, and write diary entries after sessions. It teaches behavior in-band through a normal tool call.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8426:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of the strongest API-surface ideas in the repo. Public already has `session_bootstrap`, dynamic startup instructions, and routing guidance, but MemPalace’s version is more compact and behaviorally direct.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8427:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8428:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8430:### Finding 4: Host/profile separation is shallow in source; multi-client support is partly documentation, not runtime implementation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8432:- **What it does**: The repo markets Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and Gemini compatibility, but the implemented hook harness split only recognizes `claude-code` and `codex`. Gemini support is pushed into an example/setup guide rather than a real runtime branch. Public, by contrast, already has explicit runtime separation and transport indirection in the compact-code-graph plugin to keep host/runtime boundaries clean.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8433:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s cross-client story is weaker in code than in docs. Public should not copy the “one README, many hosts” framing unless each host path is source-backed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8434:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8435:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8437:### Finding 5: The duplicate-check API is advisory, while write correctness is left to caller discipline
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8439:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8440:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest tool-contract weakness in MemPalace’s write surface. For Public, the takeaway is to keep duplicate/quality checks on the authoritative save path, not as optional caller behavior.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8441:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8442:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8444:### Finding 6: MemPalace’s wake-up/search layers are real, but they are API-adjacent utilities rather than first-class MCP profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8446:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes L0/L1/L2/L3 through `layers.py` and the CLI, but the MCP layer does not expose those as distinct profiles or modes; it mostly exposes search, taxonomy, KG, and diary tools. Public’s `memory_context` and `memory_search` do expose explicit modes and profiles (`auto`, `deep`, `resume`, `research`, `debug`) directly in the API contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8447:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable piece is the conceptual layering, not the surface design. Public already has the stronger profile-separated API surface; MemPalace mainly reinforces that startup and retrieval modes should stay user-visible and tool-visible.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8448:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8449:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8482:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8483:### Finding 1: The real CLI surface is broader than the README, and `init` does not execute the guided onboarding path it advertises
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8485:- **What it does**: The documented CLI presents `init` as guided onboarding plus AAAK bootstrap, but `cmd_init()` only runs entity scan, room detection, and config init; the actual interactive onboarding flow lives separately in `run_onboarding()` and is never called by the CLI entrypoint. The shipped CLI also includes undocumented operational subcommands like `hook run` and `instructions`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8486:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a source-confirmed drift between product story and real command surface. For Public, the reusable lesson is not “copy the README UX,” but “keep bootstrap commands honest and keep operational subcommands explicit.”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8487:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8488:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8490:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s MCP server is a thin stdin/stdout JSON-RPC transport with a single flat tool registry
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8492:- **What it does**: MemPalace negotiates `initialize`, serves `tools/list`, and dispatches `tools/call` from one `TOOLS` dict of 19 entries, coercing basic numeric types and returning each result as JSON text. Logs go to `stderr`, protocol payloads go to `stdout`, which is handshake-clean. By contrast, Public’s current MCP surface is schema-driven and much broader, with dozens of typed tool definitions and centralized dispatch.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8493:- **Why it matters for us**: The good idea here is transport simplicity and stderr discipline. The limitation is that capability separation, response shaping, and policy routing all live outside the protocol layer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8494:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8495:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8497:### Finding 3: `mempalace_status` is not just health output; it is an embedded operating protocol for the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8499:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns drawer counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to call search/KG tools before answering, invalidate stale facts, and write diary entries after sessions. It teaches behavior in-band through a normal tool call.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8500:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of the strongest API-surface ideas in the repo. Public already has `session_bootstrap`, dynamic startup instructions, and routing guidance, but MemPalace’s version is more compact and behaviorally direct.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8501:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8502:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8504:### Finding 4: Host/profile separation is shallow in source; multi-client support is partly documentation, not runtime implementation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8506:- **What it does**: The repo markets Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and Gemini compatibility, but the implemented hook harness split only recognizes `claude-code` and `codex`. Gemini support is pushed into an example/setup guide rather than a real runtime branch. Public, by contrast, already has explicit runtime separation and transport indirection in the compact-code-graph plugin to keep host/runtime boundaries clean.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8507:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s cross-client story is weaker in code than in docs. Public should not copy the “one README, many hosts” framing unless each host path is source-backed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8508:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8509:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8511:### Finding 5: The duplicate-check API is advisory, while write correctness is left to caller discipline
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8513:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8514:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest tool-contract weakness in MemPalace’s write surface. For Public, the takeaway is to keep duplicate/quality checks on the authoritative save path, not as optional caller behavior.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8515:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8516:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8518:### Finding 6: MemPalace’s wake-up/search layers are real, but they are API-adjacent utilities rather than first-class MCP profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8520:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes L0/L1/L2/L3 through `layers.py` and the CLI, but the MCP layer does not expose those as distinct profiles or modes; it mostly exposes search, taxonomy, KG, and diary tools. Public’s `memory_context` and `memory_search` do expose explicit modes and profiles (`auto`, `deep`, `resume`, `research`, `debug`) directly in the API contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8521:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable piece is the conceptual layering, not the surface design. Public already has the stronger profile-separated API surface; MemPalace mainly reinforces that startup and retrieval modes should stay user-visible and tool-visible.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8522:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8523:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:1:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 “wake-up stack” is mostly presentation policy over one store, not four distinct retrieval substrates
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:3:- **What it does**: L1 scans the whole drawer collection in batches, sorts by `importance`/`emotional_weight`/`weight`, groups by room, truncates to 15 snippets and 3200 chars, and emits compact text. L3 then reuses the same Chroma query path as `searcher.py`. The “layers” mostly change formatting, caps, and filters.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable part is not a new retrieval algorithm. It is a good prompt-packaging pattern for startup and compaction survival: cheap identity, compact essential story, topic browse, deep search. Public could borrow the packaging shape without downgrading the actual retrieval backend.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:5:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8:### Finding 4: Palace graph traversal is topic-neighborhood navigation from metadata, not a substitute for code-graph or causal retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace builds a graph by scanning drawer metadata, treating rooms as nodes and shared wings/halls as edges, then traverses by shared wing overlap with a simple BFS and fuzzy room-name matching. Public’s structural routing, by contrast, explicitly sends semantic code queries to CocoIndex and structural dependency questions to `code_graph_query`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s graph is useful for “nearby topics” in memory. It cannot answer callers, imports, dependency blast radius, or code ownership questions the way Public’s code-graph can, and it is also different from Public’s causal memory lineage.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:12:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:15:### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval stack is already better factored and more inspectable than MemPalace’s single-surface search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:17:- **What it does**: Public’s `memory_search` is hybrid by default, normalizes scope, traces retrieval, exposes rerank metadata, and binds progressive disclosure to scope-aware cursors. `context-server.ts` advertises channel availability and routes semantic code search to CocoIndex and structural queries to `code_graph_query`. The compact-code-graph plugin injects deduped transport blocks during message and compaction phases.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: On retrieval architecture, Public should not collapse back to a single “drawer search” surface. MemPalace contributes a memorable vocabulary and a good browse mental model, but Public already has the better substrate split: memory retrieval, semantic code retrieval, structural code retrieval, and transport-safe continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:19:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:20:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:87:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:158:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:159:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:160:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:171:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:172:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:173:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:270:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:272:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:274:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:275:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:276:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:277:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8030:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8031:### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8033:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8034:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is weaker on recovery depth, but stronger on “one call teaches the agent how to behave.” Public already has the better lifecycle substrate; the reusable idea is a concise behavior contract on the canonical recovery surface, not a new backend.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8035:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8036:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8038:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8040:- **What it does**: MemPalace `wake_up()` is `L0 identity.txt + L1 top 15 drawers`, with `L2` doing plain `col.get()` metadata filtering and `L3` doing the same Chroma query as normal search. Public’s resume path uses anchor-scoped `memory_search`, auto-resume heuristics, structural freshness, CocoIndex availability, and a shared payload contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8041:- **Why it matters for us**: The MemPalace stack is a good human-readable startup shape, but not a deeper lifecycle system than what Public already has. The reusable part is the compact “identity + essential story + on-demand deepening” packaging, not the underlying retrieval/state model.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8042:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8043:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8045:### Finding 3: MemPalace’s strongest lifecycle contribution is forced save timing before loss, not better saved-state structure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8047:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s Stop hook blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL` human turns and its PreCompact hook always blocks, emitting a system reason that tells the agent to save before stopping or compaction. Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8048:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage lifecycle idea in the repo. Public already survives compaction structurally, but MemPalace addresses a different failure mode: the user or agent simply forgetting to persist key session context before it disappears.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8049:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8050:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8052:### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8054:- **What it does**: MemPalace tracks save cycles with `stop_hook_active`, transcript counts, session IDs, and per-session last-save files under `~/.mempalace/hook_state/`. Public resolves trusted session IDs, preserves session lifecycle metadata, recovers interrupted sessions, recovers pending memory files on startup, and resets incomplete ingest jobs after crashes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8055:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not simplify its lifecycle state down to MemPalace’s hook-counter model. If we copy the hook timing idea, it should sit on top of the current trusted session/recovery stack rather than replace it.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8056:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8057:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8059:### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8061:- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8062:- **Why it matters for us**: For lifecycle correctness, Public’s save path is currently better. MemPalace’s free-form save timing is useful, but its save semantics are less auditable than our structured context handoff.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8063:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8064:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8102:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8103:### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8105:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8106:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is weaker on recovery depth, but stronger on “one call teaches the agent how to behave.” Public already has the better lifecycle substrate; the reusable idea is a concise behavior contract on the canonical recovery surface, not a new backend.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8107:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8108:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8110:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8112:- **What it does**: MemPalace `wake_up()` is `L0 identity.txt + L1 top 15 drawers`, with `L2` doing plain `col.get()` metadata filtering and `L3` doing the same Chroma query as normal search. Public’s resume path uses anchor-scoped `memory_search`, auto-resume heuristics, structural freshness, CocoIndex availability, and a shared payload contract.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8113:- **Why it matters for us**: The MemPalace stack is a good human-readable startup shape, but not a deeper lifecycle system than what Public already has. The reusable part is the compact “identity + essential story + on-demand deepening” packaging, not the underlying retrieval/state model.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8114:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.

If we copy the hook timing idea, it should sit on top of the current trusted session/recovery stack rather than replace it.

MemPalace’s free-form save timing is useful, but its save semantics are less auditable than our structured context handoff.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:1:### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:3:- **What it does**: MemPalace tracks save cycles with `stop_hook_active`, transcript counts, session IDs, and per-session last-save files under `~/.mempalace/hook_state/`. Public resolves trusted session IDs, preserves session lifecycle metadata, recovers interrupted sessions, recovers pending memory files on startup, and resets incomplete ingest jobs after crashes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not simplify its lifecycle state down to MemPalace’s hook-counter model. If we copy the hook timing idea, it should sit on top of the current trusted session/recovery stack rather than replace it.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:8:### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: For lifecycle correctness, Public’s save path is currently better. MemPalace’s free-form save timing is useful, but its save semantics are less auditable than our structured context handoff.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:12:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:13:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:76:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:147:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:148:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:149:- Recommendation: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:160:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:161:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:162:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:259:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:261:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:263:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:264:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:265:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:266:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:4935:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/retrieval-telemetry.ts:296:  t.quality.boostImpactDelta = Number.isFinite(boostDelta) ? boostDelta : 0;

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10786:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10787:### Finding 1: MemPalace duplicate hygiene is a thin similarity check; Public treats duplicates as a scoped save-integrity problem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10789:- **What it does**: MemPalace checks only the top 5 vector neighbors of the proposed content and flags a duplicate when `1 - distance >= threshold`; there is no exact-hash pass, no scope-aware duplicate boundary, and no layered content-quality gate before filing. Public runs exact-hash duplicate checks inside scope, similar-duplicate warnings, and a 3-layer quality gate before save.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10790:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest hygiene gap in MemPalace. Its model is good at “roughly the same text already exists,” but weak at “is this a safe, in-scope, high-quality memory write.”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10791:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10792:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10794:### Finding 2: MemPalace retrieval hygiene relies on agent obedience; Public encodes more hygiene directly into trigger recall
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10796:- **What it does**: MemPalace teaches the agent “search first, never guess,” but the implementation behind that guidance is mostly raw Chroma queries plus optional `wing`/`room` filters and an L1 wake-up summary built from top-weight drawers. Public’s trigger path validates trusted session IDs, fails closed on scope-filter errors, applies decay, classifies tiers, and only injects tier-appropriate content.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10797:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s protocol is valuable, but its hygiene lives in instructions more than enforcement. Public already has the stronger enforcement model for safe recall.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10798:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10799:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10801:### Finding 3: MemPalace health tooling is mostly “count and rebuild”; Public has bounded, confirmation-gated repair hygiene
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10803:- **What it does**: MemPalace MCP `status` reports total drawers plus wing/room counts; CLI `repair` backs up the palace, deletes the collection, and rebuilds it from extracted drawers. Public `memory_health` checks schema state, FTS sync, alias divergence, provider readiness, and only runs repair after explicit confirmation, with targeted actions for FTS rebuild, trigger-cache refresh, orphan-edge cleanup, and vector/chunk cleanup.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10804:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is operator-usable, but not hygiene-rich. Public’s health tooling is materially better for safe maintenance because it diagnoses specific failure classes instead of jumping straight to rebuild.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10805:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10806:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10808:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10810:- **What it does**: MemPalace has a separate SQLite triple store with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, manual invalidation, and `source_closet` provenance, but its own README says contradiction wiring is still pending. Public’s causal layer is less fact-like, yet it validates relation types, clamps strengths to finite `0..1`, caps auto-edge strength, clears graph caches on mutation, cleans orphaned edges, and reports graph coverage.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10811:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace wins on fact expressiveness, Public wins on operational hygiene. The reusable idea is temporal validity on edges, not the current MemPalace fact pipeline as-is.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10812:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10813:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10815:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s “store everything” story is selectively lossy in the ingest pipeline, which weakens its hygiene claims
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10817:- **What it does**: Conversation ingest truncates AI responses to the first 8 lines, room assignment is keyword-scored, heuristic extraction only keeps paragraphs that clear a confidence floor, and project re-mines delete and reinsert prior drawers. That is not “unfiltered raw memory”; it is selective routing and reduction. Public is also selective, but it makes that explicit through trigger filtering and save quality controls instead of presenting the result as untouched raw capture.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10818:- **Why it matters for us**: Hygiene depends on truthfulness about loss and transformation. MemPalace’s real pipeline is workable, but its storage story is cleaner in prose than in code.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10819:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10820:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10822:### Finding 6: MemPalace diary storage knowingly trades recall hygiene for compression convenience
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10824:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s diary tool explicitly notes that raw AAAK is stored even though compressed AAAK degrades embedding quality. Public has no direct equivalent diary feature, but its save path already expects validation and quality gating before long-term indexing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10825:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever add agent diaries, this is the exact hygiene pitfall to avoid: compressed recall artifacts should not become the primary indexed representation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10826:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10827:- **Impact**: medium
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10862:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10863:### Finding 1: MemPalace duplicate hygiene is a thin similarity check; Public treats duplicates as a scoped save-integrity problem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10865:- **What it does**: MemPalace checks only the top 5 vector neighbors of the proposed content and flags a duplicate when `1 - distance >= threshold`; there is no exact-hash pass, no scope-aware duplicate boundary, and no layered content-quality gate before filing. Public runs exact-hash duplicate checks inside scope, similar-duplicate warnings, and a 3-layer quality gate before save.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10866:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest hygiene gap in MemPalace. Its model is good at “roughly the same text already exists,” but weak at “is this a safe, in-scope, high-quality memory write.”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10867:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10868:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10870:### Finding 2: MemPalace retrieval hygiene relies on agent obedience; Public encodes more hygiene directly into trigger recall
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10872:- **What it does**: MemPalace teaches the agent “search first, never guess,” but the implementation behind that guidance is mostly raw Chroma queries plus optional `wing`/`room` filters and an L1 wake-up summary built from top-weight drawers. Public’s trigger path validates trusted session IDs, fails closed on scope-filter errors, applies decay, classifies tiers, and only injects tier-appropriate content.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10873:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s protocol is valuable, but its hygiene lives in instructions more than enforcement. Public already has the stronger enforcement model for safe recall.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10874:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10875:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10877:### Finding 3: MemPalace health tooling is mostly “count and rebuild”; Public has bounded, confirmation-gated repair hygiene
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10879:- **What it does**: MemPalace MCP `status` reports total drawers plus wing/room counts; CLI `repair` backs up the palace, deletes the collection, and rebuilds it from extracted drawers. Public `memory_health` checks schema state, FTS sync, alias divergence, provider readiness, and only runs repair after explicit confirmation, with targeted actions for FTS rebuild, trigger-cache refresh, orphan-edge cleanup, and vector/chunk cleanup.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10880:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is operator-usable, but not hygiene-rich. Public’s health tooling is materially better for safe maintenance because it diagnoses specific failure classes instead of jumping straight to rebuild.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10881:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10882:- **Impact**: high
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10884:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10886:- **What it does**: MemPalace has a separate SQLite triple store with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, manual invalidation, and `source_closet` provenance, but its own README says contradiction wiring is still pending. Public’s causal layer is less fact-like, yet it validates relation types, clamps strengths to finite `0..1`, caps auto-edge strength, clears graph caches on mutation, cleans orphaned edges, and reports graph coverage.

The reusable idea is temporal validity on edges, not the current MemPalace fact pipeline as-is.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10893:- **What it does**: Conversation ingest truncates AI responses to the first 8 lines, room assignment is keyword-scored, heuristic extraction only keeps paragraphs that clear a confidence floor, and project re-mines delete and reinsert prior drawers. That is not “unfiltered raw memory”; it is selective routing and reduction. Public is also selective, but it makes that explicit through trigger filtering and save quality controls instead of presenting the result as untouched raw capture.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10894:- **Why it matters for us**: Hygiene depends on truthfulness about loss and transformation. MemPalace’s real pipeline is workable, but its storage story is cleaner in prose than in code.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10895:- **Recommendation**: reject
